pattern: Two halves of an array can be woven together in place by walking both halves in lockstep and alternately emitting one element from each.
hints: The array is really two equal-length runs back to back; think of them as two separate pointers rather than one array.
Build the result left to right, and for each output pair pull the next unused element from the first half then the next from the second half.
For i from 0 to n-1, the output at position 2*i is nums[i] and the output at position 2*i+1 is nums[n+i].

edge_inputs.description: n at the small end with distinct halves
descending first half, ascending second half — order must follow index, not value
all identical values still interleave positionally

stress.description: random arrays across the allowed n range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 500)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(2 * n)]
    return [nums, n]
stress.note: size drives n directly; generator caps n at the stated constraint
